story.iWant: I want login with session cookies and roles (admin/manager/viewer)
story.soThat: so that access is enforced per role across the app.

task.title: Configure NextAuth provider and session settings

subtasks.subtask: Add provider config, session cookie flags (secure, httpOnly, sameSite), and callback to attach role claim.
Add startup guard to fail if required auth env vars missing.
Add role field/mapping in Prisma schema and seed minimal users/roles for dev.
Document role mapping table for admin/manager/viewer.
Add middleware/policy helper to enforce roles on protected routes; default deny.
Guard admin-only endpoints and ensure viewer routes stay read-only.

task.title: Auditing and logging for auth actions

subtasks.subtask: Record auth-related access violations and successful role checks with requestId/context.
Add integration tests for sign-in session creation and role claims.
Add tests for 403 on admin endpoints for manager/viewer and allow for admin.
Add tests ensuring viewer paths are read-only and no write affordances.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a configured NextAuth provider, when a known user signs in, then a session cookie is set and user has a role.
When a manager accesses admin-only endpoints, then access is denied with 403.
When a viewer accesses read-only pages, then access succeeds without write affordances.
Session cookies are httpOnly/secure and role is enforced in API guards.
Metrics: 100% protected routes enforce role; 0 unauthorized writes observed in audit.

doc.title: Architecture
doc.snippet: Architecture centers on a hybrid API (GraphQL for reads, REST for uploads/webhooks/health) on Next.js 16 + React 19 with PostgreSQL/Prisma and role-scoped access. RBAC via NextAuth sessions and audited writes enforces admin/manager/viewer boundaries.

doc.title: Product Requirements
doc.snippet: Roles: admins have full write, team managers have limited write for their team, and viewers are read-only. MVP excludes payments/notifications and focuses on draft board, schedules, stats upload/display, and roster/trade management.

doc.title: Epics Breakdown
doc.snippet: Story 1.2 requires session cookies with roles (admin/manager/viewer) and enforcement in API guards. Acceptance criteria cover role assignment on sign-in, 403 for manager on admin endpoints, viewer read-only access, secure httpOnly cookies, and 100% protected routes enforcing roles.

codeArtifact.reason: NextAuth configuration uses JWT sessions, Google provider, secure httpOnly cookies, and attaches user id/name/role to JWT/session for RBAC.
Auth route wiring for NextAuth server handler; entry point for session issuance.
Auth utilities for getting server session, fetching user, and enforcing role with structured authz logging and 401/403 errors.
GraphQL resolvers wrap mutations/queries with admin-only guard via assertRole; shows current access pattern and enforcement point.
Role enum defines ADMIN, MANAGER, PLAYER and is persisted for users; current role model to align with RBAC.
Env loader requires NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, and Google OAuth keys; missing vars fail fast.

dependency.reason: Auth stack built on Next.js app router with NextAuth 4.x (session cookies/JWT), Prisma/Postgres backing users/roles.

constraints.constraint: Secure cookie posture required: httpOnly, sameSite=lax, secure in production (see authOptions cookies and architecture doc).
Deny-by-default RBAC: handlers/resolvers must call assertRole; admin-only mutations currently enforced via withAdmin wrapper.
Env completeness required: NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET must be present or startup fails.
Audit/logging expectations: auth/authz events logged with requestId; extend to access violation and session issuance.
Role model alignment: Prisma Role enum is ADMIN/MANAGER/PLAYER; map story roles (admin/manager/viewer) accordingly or adjust enum.

interface.name: NextAuth session (JWT strategy)
interface.signature: Session.user: { id: string; role: Role; name?: string; email?: string }

interface.name: GraphQL admin guard
interface.signature: withAdmin(resolver) → resolver enforcing Role.ADMIN via assertRole

tests.standards: Use Jest with @testing-library for UI and integration, ts-jest for TS support, and Next.js test app pattern for route/resolver tests. Cover RBAC at handler/resolver entry with deny-by-default checks; ensure session cookie security (httpOnly/secure) and logging expectations.

ideas.idea: Sign-in flow issues JWT session with role attached; cookie flags correct (httpOnly, secure in prod, sameSite=lax); role present on session.user.
Manager hitting admin-only mutation or REST path receives 403; authz log records deny with allowed roles.
Viewer can access read-only queries/pages; write attempts blocked with 403; no write affordances in UI if applicable.
API guards enforce role for protected routes/resolvers; assertRole denies on missing/invalid roles; cookies remain httpOnly/secure.
Coverage check: all protected routes/mutations exercise role enforcement; audit/log entries captured for allow/deny; no unauthorized writes observed in test fixtures.
